title: Android App
description: Features, BLE scanning, and local data storage on Android devices.

sections.body: The Plantgotchi Android app is built with Jetpack Compose and Material 3, providing a modern experience with full offline support.

sections.heading: Key Features
sections.body:
• BLE sensor scanning — discover sensors using Android's BLE APIs
  • Real-time readings — live moisture, temperature, light, and battery data
  • Offline-first — local Room database (SQLite) stores all data
  • Cloud sync — push and pull data from Turso when connected
  • Care logging — track watering, fertilizing, repotting, pruning, and notes
  • Rule engine — instant offline alerts when readings go out of range

sections.heading: BLE Scanning
sections.body:
The Android app scans for Plantgotchi sensors advertising the custom GATT service UUID (0000FE00-...). The BLEManager handles connection lifecycle, and SensorParser converts raw BLE bytes into typed readings.

Data parsing follows the same protocol: moisture (uint16 LE), temperature (int16 LE ÷ 100), light (uint32 LE), and battery (uint8). Out-of-bounds values return null.

sections.heading: Local Database
sections.body: The app uses Room (Android's SQLite abstraction) with entity classes for Plant, SensorReading, CareLog, and Recommendation. DAO classes provide typed queries, and the schema matches the web and iOS platforms exactly for seamless cloud sync.

sections.heading: Offline Rule Engine
sections.body: The Android app includes a built-in RuleEngine that evaluates every sensor reading against your plant's thresholds. Alerts are generated instantly — no internet needed. Rules cover moisture (low/high), temperature (low/high), battery level, and light preference mismatches.

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.body:
• Android 8.0 (API 26) or later
  • Bluetooth and location permissions for BLE scanning
  • Internet only needed for cloud sync and AI recommendations
